question: I am writing a Megatron-LM sbatch skeleton for multi-node training. Answer in at most 8 bullets: what exact distributed variables and torch.distributed.run flags belong in the minimal skeleton, and what are the CUDA_DEVICE_MAX_CONNECTIONS rules for Hopper/Ampere, Blackwell/GB200, FSDP, and overlap_moe_expert_parallel_comm? You may read the relevant skill, but do not submit a job.
ground_truth: The response should use the run-on-slurm skill and include the minimal skeleton details: shared worktree, #SBATCH --nodes, --ntasks-per-node=1, --gpus-per-node, log output, cd to the worktree, MASTER_ADDR from scontrol show hostnames $SLURM_JOB_NODELIST | head -n1, MASTER_PORT, NNODES=$SLURM_NNODES, GPUS_PER_NODE, WORLD_SIZE=$((NNODES * GPUS_PER_NODE)), and srun uv run python -m torch.distributed.run with --nnodes $NNODES, --nproc-per-node $GPUS_PER_NODE, --node-rank from SLURM_NODEID, --master-addr $MASTER_ADDR, --master-port $MASTER_PORT, and the training script. It should state that all nodes must see the same shared filesystem path, and bare torchrun should be avoided in favor of uv run python -m torch.distributed.run. CUDA_DEVICE_MAX_CONNECTIONS rules: Blackwell/GB200 does not need special handling; pre-Blackwell Hopper/Ampere with TP>1 or CP>1 and non-FSDP sets it to 1; Torch-FSDP2 or Megatron-FSDP must not set it to 1 and should leave it unset or greater than 1; overlap_moe_expert_parallel_comm sets it to 32.
expected_behavior: Uses the run-on-slurm skill guidance before answering.
Names MASTER_ADDR, MASTER_PORT, NNODES, GPUS_PER_NODE, WORLD_SIZE, and the torch.distributed.run node-rank/master address flags.
Mentions the shared worktree requirement and uv run python -m torch.distributed.run.
Gives the four CUDA_DEVICE_MAX_CONNECTIONS rules for Blackwell, Hopper/Ampere non-FSDP, FSDP, and overlap_moe_expert_parallel_comm.
